chapter_title.en: Chapter 107 – The merits for the poor Muslims

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/366)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/366)
text: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَا مَعْشَرَ الْمَسَاكِينِ طِيبُوا نَفْساً وَ أَعْطُوا اللَّهَ الرِّضَا مِنْ قُلُوبِكُمْ يُثِبْكُمُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى فَقْرِكِمْ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا فَلَا ثَوَابَ لَكُمْ .

en.hubeali: ‘He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Prophet<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘O group of the poor ones! Pleasant yourselves and give the pleasure to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> from your hearts, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic will Reward you upon your poverty. But, if you do not do it, so there would be no Rewards for you’.
en.sarwar: 14. Through the same chain of narrators he has said the following: “The Holy Prophet has said, ‘O destitute people, rejoice and be happy wholeheartedly with Allah. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will reward you because of your poverty, if you did not do so, you will not receive any rewards.”
